LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI'S MOTION TO STAY JAMS ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED. A STAY UNDER CCP 916 IS NOT WARRANTED. THE CURRENT JAMS ARBITRATION PROCEEDING WILL NOT "SUBSTANTIALLY INTERFERE WITH THE APPELLATE COURT'S ABILITY TO CONDUCT THE APPEAL." VARIAN MEDICAL SYSTEMS, INC. V. DELFINO (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 180, 189-190. THE CURRENT JAMS ARBITRATION ARISES OUT OF EVENTS THAT ARE UNRELATED TO THE CLAIMS IN THIS CASE, AND THAT OCCURRED AFTER THE NOTICE OF APPEAL WAS FILED. THE CLAIMS BEFORE THE ARBITRATOR ARE THEREFORE NOT THE SUBJECT OF THE APPEAL. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S CONCERN THAT THE ARBITRATION MUST BE STAYED BECAUSE THERE IS A RISK OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS AS BETWEEN THE TRIAL COURT AND THE ARBITRATOR (IN THE EVENT THE COURT OF APPEAL UPHOLDS THE TRIAL COURT'S ORDER) IS TOO SPECULATIVE AT THIS STAGE TO WARRANT A STAY AND NOT A BASIS TO DO SO UNDER CCP 916. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set A Valuation Date And Appoint Appraisers
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leo LaRocca of Niven & Smith Law Offices telephone number 415-981-5451 for Plaintiff Paul Boschetti.
Jeffrey Makoff and Patrick Freeman of Valle Makoff LLP telephone number 415-796-0204 for all Defendants.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ADAM SPARKS, KIYOMITEX LLC, DOUBLE HORSESHOE LLC, PAC SOUTH INVESTMENTS LLC and PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS INC's Motion To Set A Valuation Date And Appoint Appraisers is denied. Because there is no pending claim for dissolution of any of the LLCs and LPs, the court lacks jurisdiction to order dissolution of the LLCs and LPs and thus there cannot be any buyout proceedings (Panakosta Partners, L.P. v. Hammer Lane Mgmt. LLC (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 612, 634; Kennedy v. Kennedy (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1474, 1491.) There is no basis to order a valuation of the alleged general partnership between the parties. The parties dispute whether a general partnership exists and thus the issue of the existence of a general partnership needs to be resolved by the trier of fact. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Stay The Winding Up And Dissolution And To Appoint Three Disinterested Appraisers Pursuant To Corporations Code
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Continued To May-02-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. LEO M. LAROCCA, ESQ. of Niven & Smith Law Offices, Tel No. 415-981-5451 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. KEVIN W. HORAN, ESQ. of Law Offices of Kevin W. Horan, Tel No. 916-599-0084 appearing for Defendant Pacific Investments.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFFS GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI AND PABO, LLC'S Motion For An Order To Stay The Winding Up And Dissolution And To Appoint Three Disinterested Appraisers Pursuant To Corporations Code,
Plaintiffs' motion for a stay of the case pending further order of the court is granted except for the further proceedings contemplated by this order. The parties are to meet and confer regarding the appointment of the three disinterested appraisers. Defendants should file a motion to set a valuation date which will be heard on May 2, 2016 or any other date agreed to by both sides. Opposition and reply papers are to be filed in accordance with CCP 1005. The motion to set valuation date should discuss the valuation date, the parties' nominees for appraisers, the meet and confer efforts, and the amount of bond pursuant to the Corporations Code. At the hearing the court will set a valuation date, determine the bond amount, and appoint appraisers. One or possibly both of plaintiff's motions for fees incurred relating to the denial of defendants' motion to compel arbitration are currently on calendar on March 10, 2016. The hearing on those motions will be continued to May 2, 2016. Today's hearing shall be continued to May 2, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion, for the sole purpose of serving as a control date. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiff Paul Boschettis Second Amended Complaint And Defendants Second Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. LEO M. LAROCCA, ESQ. of Niven & Smith Law Offices, Tel No. 415-981-5451 appearing for Plaintiffs;
Atty. JASNA VELEDAR, ESQ. of Hooshmand Law Group, Tel No. 415-318-5709 appearing for Defendants Adam Sparks and Pacific Bay Investments.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The motion of Adam Sparks and Pacific Bay Investments for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. The claims in the second amended complaint will be addressed first, then the claims in the second amended cross-complaint. Second Amended Complaint. The motion is denied as to first and second "causes of action" because a request for injunctive relief is a remedy, not a cause of action that can be adjudicated under CCP 437c(f)(1). The trial judge will weigh the evidence and decide whether injunctive relief is appropriate. The motion is denied as to third cause of action because there are triable issues regarding the existence of a fiduciary relationship based on a principal/agent relationship and whether Pacific Bay breached its duty by paying itself $10,400 per month. (Boschetti Declaration 81:1-25). The motion is denied as the fourth cause of action because there are triable issues whether Pacific Bay acted illegally by acting without a license, the claim is time-barred because the conduct is ongoing, and plaintiffs are entitled to an injunction or restitution under the UCL. The motion is denied as to fifth cause of action because plaintiffs can seek to inspect the corporate documents under Corporations Code 17708.08 and 17704.10. The motion is denied as to the sixth cause of action because the request for declaratory relief is proper to determine whether the parties entered into the "handwritten master partnership agreement." The motion is denied as to the seventh through fifteenth and nineteenth and twentieth causes because those claims are directed towards defendants that did not file the motion. Only Mr. Sparks and Pacific Bay filed the motion. The motion is denied as to the sixteenth, seventeenth and eighteenth causes of action 16, 17, and 18 because there is a triable issue whether defendants breached their tort and contractual duties by allowing the $10,400 per month fee. The motion is denied as to the twenty-first cause of action based on the $10,400 per month payment. The motion is denied as to the twenty-second cause of action because the accounts are so complicated that an ordinary legal action demanding a fixed sum is impracticable. (Los Defensores, Inc. v. Gomez (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 377, 401). Second Amended Cross-complaint. The motion is denied as to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth and seventh causes of action because there are triable issues regarding what agreement controls the parties' relationship and what sums, if any, are due. The motion is denied as to the sixth cause of action because it is unclear whether Sparks has an ownership interest in the cited properties and is entitled to an accounting. The motion is denied as to the eighth cause of action because there is a triable issue regarding what work Sparks performed and the reasonable value of that work. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LEO M. LAROCCA OF NIVEN & SMITH LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-5451 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JASNA VELEDAR OF THE HOOSHMAND LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-415-318-5709 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.
RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI AND PABO, LLC'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. A preliminary injunction requires a showing of great or irreparable harm; when that showing is made, the Court must then consider the likelihood that the moving party will ultimately prevail on the merits and the relative harm to the parties from issuance or nonissuance of the injunction. (Cosa Mesa City Employees' Assn v. City of Costa Mesa (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 298, 306.) The purpose of a preliminary injunction "is to preserve the status quo until a final determination following a trial." (Id. at 305.) Here, the continued management of the property and deduction of management fees by Defendants is not the sort of great or irreparable injury which justifies a preliminary injunction. The fact that Boschetti waited over five years after the filing of this suit to bring his motion for preliminary injunction illustrates that this is not the sort of emergency situation in which judicial intervention is warranted. Boschetti does not allege that Defendants are insolvent or that monetary damages after trial will in any other way be in inadequate legal remedy. Furthermore, issuance of a preliminary injunction would leave the properties without any management; it would not further the purpose of "preserving the status quo until a final determination following a trial." (See Fretz v. Burke (1967) 247 Cal.App.2d 741, 746 (preliminary injunction requiring continued distributions of partnership income valid as preserving the status quo when defendants been illegally diverting the funds after litigation was commenced).) The Court also does not believe Plaintiff has shown a likelihood of success on the merits as to these claims. However, the Court GRANTS Plaintiff's request for a preliminary injunction immediately enjoining Defendants from using Plaintiff's share of partnership assets to pay for Defendants' legal defense in this action from this point forward. This admitted use of Plaintiff's share of the partnership assets to pay for the legal defense of his own lawsuit has brought appears to amount to misappropriation of partnership assets. (See Oakdale Village Group v. Fong (1996) 43 Cal.App.4th 539, 545-46.) This amounts to an irreparable injury which Plaintiff should not be forced to bear; Plaintiff is essentially being forced to bankroll both sides of his own litigation. Moreover, granting this portion of the preliminary injunction serves the purpose of preserving the status quo pending resolution of the claim. (See Cosa Mesa, supra, 209 Cal.App.4th at 305.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LEO LAROCCA OF THE NIVEN SMITH LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 981-5451 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JASNA VELEDAR OF THE HOOSHMAND LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 318-5709 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI aka G. PAUL BOSCHETTI'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED CROSS- COMPLAINT OF ADAMS SPARKS AND PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS, INCORPORATED IS Granted in part, with 20 days leave to amend. The following language is stricken from the First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC"): (1) p. 14:3, the words "and for which Pacific Bay was entitled to commission"; (2) p. 14:14, the words "and under which Pacific Bay was entitled to commission" (3) p. 20:21, the words "and Pacific Bay;" (4) p. 24:23, the words "and Pacific Bay;" and (5) p. 26:27, the word "commissions." Cross-Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The Court takes judicial notice of the fact that Pacific Bay Investments, Inc. is not licensed as a real estate broker in California, Texas or Hawaii. The FACC alleges that Pacific Bay Investments, Inc. is a distinct entity; it cannot collect commissions as a matter of law because it does not have a broker's license. Leave to amend is granted to permit Cross-Complainant Adam Sparks to assert claims for commission on his individual behalf, rather than on behalf of the corporation. (See In re Estate of Baldwin (1973) 34 Cal.App.3d 596, holding that the licensed broker can recover even though the contract provided that his corporation would be paid the commission.) The motion to strike is denied as to the remaining allegations. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion To Stay Arbitration Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LEO M. LAROCA OF THE NIVEN & SMITH LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. 981-5451 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER R. BOLDIG OF THE HOOSHMAND LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 318-5709 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI'S Motion To Stay JAMS Arbitration Proceedings is denied. A stay under CCP 916 is not warranted. The current JAMS arbitration proceeding will not "substantially interfere with the appellate court's ability to conduct the appeal." Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 189-190. The current JAMS arbitration arises out of events that are unrelated to the claims in this case, and that occurred after the notice of appeal was filed. The claims before the arbitrator are therefore not the subject of the appeal. Further, Plaintiff's concern that the arbitration must be stayed because there is a risk of inconsistent rulings as between the trial court and the arbitrator (in the event the Court of Appeal upholds the trial court's order) is too speculative at this stage to warrant a stay and not a basis to do so under CCP 916. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185
